THE MAISON The house. Pénombre keeps a small house and a long bench, and makes perfume for the half-light — the hours that belong fully neither to the day nor to the dark. We are interested in the thresholds: the cold blue before dawn, the cooling of stone at dusk, the narrow stillness at midnight. What follows is not a history. It is how we work. 01 On materials We buy few things, and we buy them well. Iris that takes years to travel from rhizome to butter. The resins — frankincense, myrrh, labdanum — and the absolutes that cannot be faked. Where a natural carries the idea, we use the natural. Where a synthetic carries it further — cleaner, steadier, kinder to the world — we use the synthetic, and we say so. A perfume is judged by how it smells, not by the romance of its parts. 02 On slowness A composition lives on the bench for months before it is named. We wear it, change it, set it down, return to it. Materials marry slowly; a formula that smells finished on Monday is rarely finish

Pénombre keeps a small house and a long bench, and makes perfume for the half-light — the hours that belong fully neither to the day nor to the dark. We are interested in the thresholds: the cold blue before dawn, the cooling of stone at dusk, the narrow stillness at midnight. What follows is not a history. It is how we work.

01 On materials We buy few things, and we buy them well. Iris that takes years to travel from rhizome to butter. The resins — frankincense, myrrh, labdanum — and the absolutes that cannot be faked. Where a natural carries the idea, we use the natural. Where a synthetic carries it further — cleaner, steadier, kinder to the world — we use the synthetic, and we say so. A perfume is judged by how it smells, not by the romance of its parts. 02 On slowness A composition lives on the bench for months before it is named. We wear it, change it, set it down, return to it. Materials marry slowly; a formula that smells finished on Monday is rarely finished by Friday. Nothing here is released to a calendar. It is released when it is right, and not before. 03 On restraint The hardest decision in a formula is what to leave out. A perfume that tries to please everyone in the room ends up smelling of no one. We would rather make something quiet and exact than something loud and approximate. Each Pénomb
We are a small house. We intend to stay one.
BEGIN Five hours await. THE COLLECTION WRITE TO THE HOUSE
